title: Red Europe
description: Red Europe is a non-fiction by Frank Anstey. Banned by the Reform Government according to Opposition MP Harry Holland.
descriptionParagraphs: Red Europe is a non-fiction by Frank Anstey. Banned by the Reform Government according to Opposition MP Harry Holland.

What makes it interesting is the way a book becomes legible to officials as a political instrument rather than a neutral cultural object. As a non-fiction, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in New Zealand. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned by the Reform Government according to Opposition MP Harry Holland. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: Red Europe is a non-fiction by Frank Anstey. Banned by the Reform Government according to Opposition MP Harry Holland.
banningParagraphs: Red Europe entered censorship debates as a non-fiction associated with politics, public argument, and state power. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around political control and political dissent.

The earliest event currently captured here is 20th century in New Zealand, where New Zealand censorship authorities classified, prohibited, or restricted. Banned by the Reform Government according to Opposition MP Harry Holland. Banned by the Reform Government according to Opposition MP Harry Holland.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
